title: Momentum Duration Detector — Regime Age Signal
summary: Track the 'age' of the current momentum regime for each stock by measuring how many consecutive days the 12-month return has remained positive (or negative). As duration increases beyond 8 months, reduce momentum signal weight and simultaneously increase mean-reversion signal weight — implementing the semi-Markov insight that state termination probability increases with age. This is distinct from HMM regime detection (which uses volatility clustering) because it uses return duration, not volatility state.
expected_impact: Giner & Zakamulin (Economic Modelling 2023, Journal of Asset Management 2024) show that momentum lasts ~12 months then reverses; their semi-Markov model correctly predicts duration-dependent state termination. Detecting when a momentum trend is 'old' (>8 months) should reduce momentum false positives by an estimated 15-20% and improve mean-reversion entry timing.
